cmd.purpose: Organisational root for all seven deployable binary entrypoints. Each child is a thin Wire+main.go launcher that delegates all business logic to openmeter/* domain packages; no domain logic or shared state lives here.

patterns.name: Wire-only entrypoint
patterns.description: Each cmd/* binary contains only wire.go (provider declarations), wire_gen.go (generated), and main.go (config load + run.Group). Business logic belongs in openmeter/* or app/common.
patterns.example: cmd/server/wire.go declares wire.Build(...); cmd/server/main.go calls initializeApplication and delegates to common.Runner.

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before Run
patterns.description: Provisioning steps (EnsureBusinessAccounts, SandboxProvisioner, initNamespace) must execute after DB migration but before the run.Group starts consuming events.
patterns.example: cmd/billing-worker/main.go calls EnsureBusinessAccounts then run.Group.Run(); cmd/server/main.go calls initNamespace before adding HTTP listener actor.

patterns.name: Namespace handler registration before initNamespace
patterns.description: In cmd/server, all namespace.Handler implementations (Ledger, KafkaIngest) must be registered via manager.RegisterHandler before initNamespace is called so the default namespace receives all handler provisioning.
patterns.example: cmd/server/main.go registers ledger handler then kafkaIngest handler then calls initNamespace(ctx, app.NamespaceManager).

patterns.name: common.Runner / oklog run.Group for actor lifecycle
patterns.description: Long-running actors (HTTP server, Kafka consumer, telemetry server) are added to run.Group via common.Runner or manually with run.Add. No goroutine is spawned outside run.Group.

patterns.name: Metadata via common.NewMetadata with binary name
patterns.description: Each binary calls common.NewMetadata(version, binaryName) to populate OTel service.name and log fields. Version is read from ldflags or runtime/debug fallback.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the Wire provider graph for the binary. Contains //go:build wireinject and wire.Build call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never remove //go:build wireinject. Adding a new domain service requires adding its provider set to wire.Build here.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by `go generate ./...` from wire.go. Do not edit manually.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any manual edit is overwritten on next `make generate`. Regenerate after every wire.go or constructor signature change.

key_file_guides.role: Largest main.go: registers namespace handlers, provisions default namespace and billing profile, builds router.Config, assembles run.Group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Namespace handler registration order is load-bearing. KafkaIngest must follow Ledger. Both must precede initNamespace.

key_file_guides.role: Provisions ledger business accounts and sandbox app before starting the Watermill consumer loop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EnsureBusinessAccounts and SandboxProvisioner must complete before run.Group.Run().

key_file_guides.role: Constructs Watermill Kafka subscriber in main.go (not Wire) so consumer group name can come from config at startup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not move subscriber construction into Wire. Use app.EventPublisher.Marshaler() for event deserialization, not app.MessagePublisher.

key_file_guides.role: Wires the entire application once via PersistentPreRunE; sub-commands use internal.App package-level globals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not construct domain services inline in RunE. Always use cmd.Context() — never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Thin launcher: blank-imports plugin packages then calls service.RunCLI. No Wire DI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New collector plugins must be added as blank imports here. Do not add Wire or domain service instantiation.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic directly in main.go instead of openmeter/* domain packages
Manually editing wire_gen.go — always regenerate via `make generate`
Registering a namespace.Handler after initNamespace in cmd/server
Constructing domain services (billing, charges, ledger) inline in cmd/jobs RunE instead of using internal.App
Introducing context.Background() anywhere other than the root context at program start in main()

decisions.decision: All business logic lives in openmeter/* or app/common; cmd/* is wiring and lifecycle only.
decisions.rationale: Keeps domain packages independently testable and reusable across binaries without importing cmd/*.

decisions.decision: Each binary has its own Wire provider graph in wire.go rather than sharing a single graph.
decisions.rationale: Different binaries need different subsets of providers; separate graphs prevent unintended coupling and reduce binary size.

decisions.decision: cmd/notification-service constructs its Watermill subscriber in main.go, not in Wire.
decisions.rationale: Consumer group name must come from config at startup, which is not available during Wire's compile-time graph resolution.

collector.purpose: Houses the Benthos/Redpanda Connect collector plugin surface (collector/benthos) and local demo environment (collector/quickstart). collector/benthos defines all custom Go plugins (input, bloblang, internal utils, leader-election service) that cmd/benthos-collector imports via blank imports; no business logic from openmeter/* is wired here.

patterns.name: Blank-import activation for plugin registration
patterns.description: Benthos plugins must be registered in init() before process start. cmd/benthos-collector imports sub-packages as _ to trigger init-time registration.

patterns.name: Leader-election gate on all replicated work
patterns.description: All work that must not run on every replica checks services/leaderelection.IsLeader(res). Absent key defaults to true (safe solo deployment).
patterns.example: if !leaderelection.IsLeader(res) { return nil } // skip work on non-leader replicas

patterns.name: Environment-variable substitution in YAML presets
patterns.description: All YAML presets under presets/ and quickstart/ use ${VAR:default} substitution. No values are hardcoded.

patterns.name: Child-package specialisation
patterns.description: input/, bloblang/, internal/, services/, presets/ each own exactly one concern. Cross-cutting utils go to internal/ only.
patterns.example: collector/benthos/internal/shutdown/signaller.go — shutdown signal utility, not a plugin.

key_file_guides.role: Leader-election state stored in service.Resources generic map; IsLeader(res) helper reads it with absent-key=true default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never read IsLeaderKey directly via res.GetGeneric — use IsLeader(res) to preserve absent-key=true semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Side-effect-only package: registers bloblang functions in init(). No exported symbols.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not export symbols from this package. Do not place business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Internal message transaction utility. Pure utilities layer — no plugin registrations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add plugin registrations to internal/. Keep it as a pure utility layer.

key_file_guides.role: Demo stack that includes ../../quickstart/docker-compose.yaml rather than duplicating service definitions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not duplicate service definitions. Use in-memory dedupe cache (not Redis) in quickstart.

anti_patterns: Registering a plugin outside init() — Benthos requires init-time registration before process start
Adding business logic or plugin registrations to internal/ — pure utilities layer only
Reading IsLeaderKey directly via res.GetGeneric instead of using IsLeader(res) helper
Exporting symbols from collector/benthos/bloblang — side-effect-only package
Hardcoding OPENMETER_URL or OPENMETER_TOKEN in YAML configs instead of environment variable interpolation

decisions.decision: Sub-package per concern (input, bloblang, internal, services, presets) rather than a flat package.
decisions.rationale: Benthos plugin types have distinct registration and lifecycle semantics; separating them prevents accidental cross-registration and clarifies what cmd/benthos-collector must blank-import.

decisions.decision: No Wire DI or openmeter domain service instantiation in collector/.
decisions.rationale: The Benthos framework provides its own component lifecycle; injecting openmeter Wire graphs would couple the collector to the full service dependency tree unnecessarily.

deploy.purpose: Kubernetes deployment artefacts for OpenMeter: two independently versioned Helm charts (openmeter, benthos-collector) published to GHCR OCI registry, plus a local kind cluster config for development. deploy/charts owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline used by both charts.

patterns.name: helm-docs via shared template
patterns.description: Both chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from deploy/charts/template.md and each chart's values.yaml comments. Never hand-edit a chart README.md.
patterns.example: make -C deploy/charts docs  # regenerates both chart READMEs

patterns.name: OCI Helm registry distribution
patterns.description: Charts are packaged and pushed to ghcr.io OCI registry on release tags, not a traditional index-based repository.

key_file_guides.role: Drives helm-docs README generation for both charts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a third chart requires a new README.tmpl.md and an update to this Makefile.

key_file_guides.role: Shared helm-docs template consumed by both child charts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changes here affect both chart READMEs. Keep template generic.

key_file_guides.role: kind cluster config for local Kubernetes development with ingress port mappings.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Port mappings (80/443) must match the Helm chart service/ingress defaults.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments
Adding a third chart without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and Makefile update
Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at the deploy/charts level — each chart must be self-contained
Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag in values.yaml

decisions.decision: Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart.
decisions.rationale: Avoids README format drift between the two charts; single edit point for documentation structure.

decisions.decision: OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index.
decisions.rationale: OCI registries provide content-addressable artefact storage and align with the container image distribution model already used for the application images.

test.purpose: Integration and cross-domain test suites that require a real PostgreSQL database. Each child owns one domain or cross-domain scenario; all wire services from raw package constructors (never app/common) to avoid import cycles. This folder is the canonical home for tests that span multiple openmeter/* packages.

patterns.name: BaseSuite embedding for shared billing infrastructure
patterns.description: Tests that need the full billing stack embed test/billing.BaseSuite (or extend it with SubscriptionMixin). BaseSuite provisions Atlas migrations, a unique namespace, MockStreamingConnector, and pkg/clock control.
patterns.example: type MyTestSuite struct { billingtest.BaseSuite } — then access s.BillingService, s.MockStreamingConnector.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for service access
patterns.description: test/notification, test/app, and test/customer expose a TestEnv interface rather than service structs directly, keeping the wiring details hidden from test methods.

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test method
patterns.description: Each test method generates a unique namespace (ULID or function-name-based) to prevent FK constraint violations when tests share a database.
patterns.example: ns := ulid.Make().String(); // or setupNamespace(t) returning a unique string

patterns.name: Raw package constructors only — no app/common imports
patterns.description: All test wiring constructs services from their direct constructors (adapter.New, service.New) not from Wire provider sets in app/common to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: clock.SetTime + defer clock.ResetTime for deterministic time
patterns.description: Tests that depend on time call pkg/clock.SetTime at the start and defer clock.ResetTime() to restore global state for parallel tests.
patterns.example: clock.SetTime(t0); defer clock.ResetTime(); // then call service methods

patterns.name: t.Context() over context.Background()
patterns.description: All new test code uses t.Context() (or s.T().Context()) so context cancellation is tied to the test harness lifecycle.

key_file_guides.role: BaseSuite: provisions Atlas migrations, unique namespace, MockStreamingConnector, billing service stack. Extended by most billing tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use BaseSuite rather than manually provisioning these — duplicating setup leads to subtle test isolation bugs.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionMixin: adds subscription+plan wiring on top of BaseSuite for tests that exercise billing+subscription flows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use SubscriptionMixin when a test needs both billing and subscription services; do not wire subscription separately.

key_file_guides.role: Wires the full notification stack including a real Svix client (requires SVIX_HOST env var).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not create a second TestEnv inside a test method — each starts goroutines and DB connections. Always defer env.Close().

key_file_guides.role: Entry point for credits/charges integration tests; uses chargestestutils.NewServices as the canonical charges stack builder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use createLedgerBackedCustomer for tests requiring ledger accounts, not BaseSuite.CreateTestCustomer.

key_file_guides.role: Wires all subscription+billing deps via direct constructors and subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps. Shared testDeps struct used by all scenario files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hardcoded namespace 'test-namespace'. Do not use a different namespace in this package.

key_file_guides.role: TestEnv for app integration tests; wires billing service via InitBillingService helper without app/common.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always call setupNamespace(t) per test method in AppHandlerTestSuite — never share a namespace across methods.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set in test files — always wire from raw package constructors to avoid import cycles
Using context.Background() where t.Context() or s.T().Context() is available
Reusing the same namespace, subject key, or customer key across test functions in the same package
Omitting defer clock.ResetTime() after clock.SetTime() — leaves global clock dirty for subsequent parallel tests
Accessing adapter methods directly when the service covers the same operation — bypasses state machine and validation

decisions.decision: Integration tests live in test/* rather than co-located with production code.
decisions.rationale: Cross-domain tests import multiple openmeter/* packages; placing them in a neutral test/* tree prevents import cycles and makes the cross-domain dependency explicit.

decisions.decision: Atlas migrations run by default in BaseSuite (not ent.Schema.Create).
decisions.rationale: Running Atlas migrations in tests validates migration files against the live schema, catching drift before CI. TEST_DISABLE_ATLAS exists as an escape hatch only.

decisions.decision: Scenario-based test files in test/subscription rather than table-driven tests.
decisions.rationale: Subscription lifecycle scenarios are complex multi-step flows where numbered step comments provide clearer documentation than a flat table of inputs/outputs.

code_examples.scenario: Extending BaseSuite for a billing+subscription integration test
